content_markdown: The behaviour of a monatomic gas such as helium will approximate to that of an ideal gas when it is kept at

options.label: A
options.text: a temperature close to absolute zero.

vibe_explanation: Real gases behave most like ideal gases at low pressures and high temperatures. At low pressure, the volume of the gas particles themselves becomes negligible compared to the total volume of the container, and the particles are far enough apart that intermolecular forces are negligible. While 'very high temperature' is also a condition for ideal behavior, 'low pressure' is a more fundamental requirement for the assumptions of the kinetic theory of gases to hold true in standard IB contexts.
